latency_source_classification.evidence: Sampled production career detail HTML consistently returned cache-control private, no-cache, no-store, max-age=0, must-revalidate.
latency_source_classification.impact: Each crawler/user request can pay SSR and backend fetch cost instead of receiving a reusable cached document.

latency_source_classification.evidence: app/(localized)/[locale]/career/jobs/[slug]/page.tsx loads the career bundle for metadata and page rendering; legacy detail rendering can also load explainability, first-wave next-step links, and runtime config.
latency_source_classification.impact: Cold or concurrent crawls can amplify backend latency across several career endpoints.

latency_source_classification.evidence: fetchCareerJobBundle(includeSeoAuthority=true) fetches /v0.5/career/jobs/{slug} and then /v0.5/career-jobs/{slug}/seo before returning the bundle.
latency_source_classification.impact: Metadata and page render wait for a serial backend dependency chain.

latency_source_classification.evidence: HTML p95 was 174822.25 bytes and max was 189877 bytes in the 200-page sample.
latency_source_classification.impact: Payload size is material but the main bottleneck is fetch/cache budget rather than static HTML size alone.

scope: Introduce explicit career detail fetch/cache budget and request de-duplication where safe.
Avoid serial metadata/page duplicate backend dependency when the same bundle can be shared or cached.
Audit whether career detail HTML must remain no-store; if not, move to bounded public/stale revalidation while preserving held slug and noindex safety.
Keep backend authority as the only content source; do not add frontend career fallback content.
